findings.title: Tools have no path to reach SessionManager or EventBus in current architecture
findings.why_it_matters: The data flow diagram shows TaskCreate.Execute() calling SessionManager and EventBus directly, but the current Tool interface only receives a context carrying the project directory. Existing tools (grep, file_read, bash) are stateless and have zero access to SessionManager or EventBus. Without addressing this injection gap, the three task tools cannot implement the specified behavior. This is not a design disagreement — the plan's architecture assumes infrastructure that does not exist.
findings.suggested_fix: Decide how tools reach session-scoped state. Options: (a) pass the *App reference into tool constructors (modifies RegisterDefaults signature and all tool constructors), (b) embed session_id and an event-emitter callback in the tool context, or (c) have the agent loop detect task tools by name and route their results through App after execution. Document the chosen approach in the spec.
evidence: `TaskCreate.Execute() → SessionManager.CreateTasks(sessionId, tasks)` — Section 1 architecture diagram
Existing tools only receive `projectDir` via constructor injection: `func NewGrep(projectDir string) tool.Tool` — internal/tool/builtin/grep.go:20
The Tool interface signature has no session/event parameters: `Execute(ctx context.Context, args json.RawMessage) (ExecutionResult, error)` — internal/tool/tool.go:13
Agent loop creates tool context with only project dir: `toolCtx := tool.WithProjectDir(ctx, a.projectDir)` — internal/agent/agent_loop.go:180

findings.title: Tasks field on SessionInfo instead of Session means tasks won't persist
findings.why_it_matters: The spec proposes adding `Tasks []Task` to `SessionInfo` (types.go:45-50), but session JSON files persist the `Session` struct (session_manager.go:24-34), not `SessionInfo`. `SessionInfo` is a view model constructed on-the-fly in `List()` — it never touches JSON persistence. If tasks are stored only on SessionInfo, they will be lost when the session is reloaded. The field must be added to the `Session` struct instead.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `Tasks []Task` to the `Session` struct in internal/api/session_manager.go, not to `SessionInfo` in internal/api/types.go. The `SessionInfo` struct can remain unchanged unless task preview is needed in the session list. The file path comment in the spec ("internal/api/session_manager.go") should reference the correct location.
evidence: `// SessionInfo 扩展 (internal/api/session_manager.go)` — Section 4 code block shows adding Tasks to SessionInfo
`type Session struct { ... }` — session_manager.go:24 — this is the persisted struct; SessionInfo at types.go:45 is the view model
`func (sm *SessionManager) Save(s *Session) error` — session_manager.go:101 saves `Session`, not `SessionInfo`
`func (sm *SessionManager) Load(id string) (*Session, error)` — session_manager.go:85 loads into `Session`
`func (sm *SessionManager) List() ([]SessionInfo, error)` — session_manager.go:123 constructs SessionInfo from Session fields at lines 140-145

findings.title: EventBus carries StreamEvent which has no task_updated type — UI won't update
findings.section: 1. Architecture & Data Flow and 5. Frontend Event Flow
findings.why_it_matters: The data flow depends on `EventBus.Push("task_updated", payload)` driving frontend Zustand store updates. But the EventBus channels carry `StreamEvent` (types.go:5-13) which has typed fields (Type: "text_delta"|"thinking"|"tool_start"|...). There is no "task_updated" event type in StreamEvent. The frontend `setupWailsEvents` (store/index.ts:661-796) dispatches on `data.type` with a finite switch-case. Without extending StreamEvent and both the Go and frontend dispatch, tasks created by the agent will be invisible to the UI. The spec marks this as "optional" in the modified files table — it is not optional.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `Tasks []Task` (or a task-change payload struct) to StreamEvent, add a `"task_updated"` case to `handleAgentEvent` in app.go:351-380, and add a corresponding `case 'task_updated'` in `setupWailsEvents` in store/index.ts:674-793. Remove the "optional" qualifier from the event.go row in Section 6.
evidence: `EventBus.Push("task_updated", payload) → 前端 Zustand store → TodoPanel 重渲染` — Section 1 architecture diagram
StreamEvent has no Tasks field: `type StreamEvent struct { Type string; Content string; ... Tool *agent.ToolEvent; ... }` — internal/api/types.go:5-13
handleAgentEvent switch-case covers only: text_delta, thinking, tool_start, tool_output, tool_done, usage, error, done, session_updated, turn_start — internal/api/app.go:351-380
Frontend switch-case covers only: text_delta, thinking, tool_start, tool_output, tool_done, usage, error, file_changed, done, session_updated, turn_start — store/index.ts:674-793
`internal/agent/event.go | 可选新增 EventTaskUpdated 事件类型` — Section 6 modified files table calls this optional

findings.title: EventBus method name mismatch: Push vs Emit
findings.why_it_matters: The spec consistently uses `EventBus.Push(...)` throughout the architecture diagram and description, but the actual API is `EventBus.Emit(ev StreamEvent)`. The existing codebase uses `Emit` exclusively. This will cause confusion during implementation and wastes time.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace all occurrences of `EventBus.Push(...)` with `EventBus.Emit(...)` in the document.
evidence: `EventBus.Push("task_updated", payload)` — Section 1 architecture diagram and description
`func (eb *EventBus) Emit(ev StreamEvent)` — internal/api/eventbus.go:44 — actual method signature
The existing codebase only calls `a.eventBus.Emit(se)` — internal/api/app.go:382

findings.title: No error handling specification for TaskUpdate on missing task ID
findings.why_it_matters: The spec defines TaskUpdate as updating a single task's fields, but doesn't specify what happens when `taskId` references a non-existent task. The LLM will inevitably generate TaskUpdate for stale or hallucinated IDs. Without defined behavior, the error path will be implemented ad-hoc (likely silently dropping the update or returning a generic error), which degrades the agent's ability to self-correct. The completeness principle applies: defining this error case costs one sentence in the spec but saves debugging cycles later.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the TaskUpdate description: "If taskId does not match any existing task, return an error listing the valid task IDs so the agent can self-correct."
evidence: `TaskUpdate: 更新单个 task 的部分字段。仅更新传入的字段，其余保持不变。` — Section 2, no error case specified
TaskUpdate table lists fields (taskId, status, subject, description, addBlockedBy) but no row about error behavior

findings.title: No validation specification for blockedBy field in TaskCreate
findings.why_it_matters: Task objects have a `blockedBy` field referencing other task IDs. The spec doesn't say whether TaskCreate should validate that blockedBy IDs exist within the submitted task list, or what happens if a task references an ID that doesn't exist. The LLM will produce circular dependencies or dangling references. Defining validation behavior now avoids producing broken dependency graphs that confuse the agent and clutter the UI.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to TaskCreate description: "All blockedBy IDs must reference other task IDs within the same tasks array. Reject with an error listing unresolved references if any blockedBy ID has no matching task."
evidence: `blockedBy | []string | no | 依赖的其他 task ID` — Section 2 TaskCreate table, no validation note
The prompt instructions state `BlockedBy expresses hard dependencies: task can't start before blockedBy tasks complete` — Section 3, but the tool definition doesn't enforce this

findings.title: Custom tool registration pathway differs from all existing tools — no pattern for session-scoped tools
findings.why_it_matters: All existing 7 builtin tools take only `projectDir` as a constructor argument and are registered once in `RegisterDefaults`. The three new task tools need SessionManager and EventBus access, which is session-scoped (different SessionManager per project). This means `RegisterDefaults` either needs a new signature (accepting App or SessionManager) or the task tools need to be registered separately after App initialization. The spec's modified files table shows `internal/tool/builtin/register.go` being modified but doesn't address this signature change.
evidence: `func RegisterDefaults(r *tool.ToolRegistry, projectDir string) error` — internal/tool/builtin/register.go:5 — only takes projectDir
All existing tool constructors take only projectDir: `NewGrep(projectDir string)`, `NewBash(projectDir string)`, etc.
Three new files (task_create.go, task_update.go, task_list.go) will need different constructor signatures than the existing 7 tools

residual_risks: Agent may over-produce task lists despite prompt instructions — the 40% height cap mitigates UI impact but excessive tool calls still consume context. Consider a hard limit on task count (e.g., max 15 tasks per session) enforced by TaskCreate itself.

TodoPanel is read-only but the LLM sees no feedback that the panel updated — the agent relies implicitly on its own tool calls succeeding. Consider emitting an event that the agent can observe (e.g., confirming the current in_progress task in the next turn's context).

Switching sessions mid-generation: the spec says TodoPanel binds to activeSessionId and generatingSessionId is independent, but if the agent is running TaskCreate/TaskUpdate on a background session, the active session's TodoPanel won't update until the user switches to that session. This is correct behavior for the current multi-tab model, but worth verifying.
deferred_questions: Should TaskList be visible to the LLM's system prompt context as a state snapshot, or is the agent expected to call it explicitly? The prompt says 'Read current status with TaskList before deciding next step' which implies explicit calls, but automatic injection of the current task state into each turn's system message could reduce tool calls.

The spec says TaskCreate always replaces the full list. If the agent inadvertently calls TaskCreate on a trivial task, it wipes the existing plan. Should TaskCreate require confirmation (via the confirmFn) for calls that would replace a non-empty list?

Should the in_progress task be persisted across session reloads with its status, or does the agent re-derive the in_progress task on the next turn? The spec says status is persisted in JSON, so re-derivation shouldn't be needed, but the prompt doesn't tell the agent to check persisted status on reload.
